steps.title: Build the Confidence Interval
steps.content:
A margin of error defines a range of plausible values for the population parameter. Substituting the given values:

$$\text{Lower bound} = 0.49 - 0.04 = 0.45$$
$$\text{Upper bound} = 0.49 + 0.04 = 0.53$$

The interval of plausible values is $[0.45, 0.53]$.

steps.title: Match to the Correct Choice
steps.content:
Checking each option:
  • Choice A: The proportion is between 0.45 and 0.53 — this matches our confidence interval exactly.
  • Choice B: Values less than 0.45 are outside (below) the interval — not plausible.
  • Choice C: The true proportion is unknown; 0.49 is the estimate, not the actual value.
  • Choice D: Values greater than 0.53 are outside (above) the interval — not plausible.
The conclusion that best describes the range of plausible values is choice A — A is correct.
